llm_extracted.ai_arm_selection_rationale: Only one AI arm available: gpt_4o_treatment using GPT-4o. Control arm received no AI assistance.
llm_extracted.notes: Sample sizes (n) are not reported in the arms data. The paper reports a 34% improvement in time to completion, which for a time-based outcome (smaller is better) represents a reduction, hence -0.34. Mean times are clearly stated: 33 minutes (AI) vs 50 minutes (control). Standard deviations are not reported.
source_quotes: On average, participants in the treatment group completed the task in 33 minutes, which was significantly shorter than the control group's average completion time of 50 minutes.
A mean difference of 17 minutes represents a 34% improvement for the treatment group in average time to complete the task compared to the control group.
The time difference was statistically significant (p < 0.05)

computed.notes: Sample sizes (n) are not reported in the arms data. The paper reports a 34% improvement in time to completion, which for a time-based outcome (smaller is better) represents a reduction, hence -0.34. Mean times are clearly stated: 33 minutes (AI) vs 50 minutes (control). Standard deviations are not reported. | SDs/SEs missing; variance not computable
